steps.title: Identify the antecedent and what form is needed
steps.content: The blank fills in "nurses ______ young with milk." The antecedent is "the animal" (singular, non-human), so the pronoun must be singular and possessive — not a contraction, not plural. The rule: a possessive pronoun must agree in number with its antecedent and must be a true possessive, not a contraction.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'they're': Pronoun-antecedent agreement error — "they're" is plural ("they are"), but "the animal" is singular; also a contraction, not a possessive. ✗
  • Choice B — 'their': Pronoun-antecedent agreement error — "their" is plural, but "the animal" is singular. ✗
  • Choice C — 'its': Singular possessive pronoun matching the singular antecedent "the animal." ✓
  • Choice D — 'it's': Contraction error — "it's" means "it is," which is not a possessive and creates an ungrammatical double-verb clause ("nurses it is young"). ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
